status_model.planned: scoped but not yet implemented in the current branch
status_model.coded: landed in code but not yet wired across the runtime/docs/tests surface
status_model.wired: connected through runtime/docs with narrower verification than release-grade gates
status_model.tested: implemented and covered by local/CI verification, but primarily operator or internal-facing
status_model.released: implemented, wired, documented, and exposed as current branch baseline behavior

capabilities.name: runtime_api_baseline
capabilities.notes: Core FastAPI runtime routes, dependency injection, auth, and health/version contracts are wired and documented.

capabilities.name: execution_and_approval_audit_kernel
capabilities.notes: Rewrite sessions persist durable artifacts, explicit steps, receipts, run costs, and approval state with legacy schema upgrade compatibility.

capabilities.name: observation_and_delivery_reliability_kernel
capabilities.notes: Observation attribution and delivery retry/idempotency fields are wired through the current runtime APIs and repositories.

capabilities.name: tool_contract_and_planner_kernel
capabilities.notes: Typed task contracts compile rewrite intent into plan metadata and drive the current orchestrator path.

capabilities.name: principal_scoped_memory_seed_apis
capabilities.notes: Principal-scoped memory seed stores and APIs are exercised by both `tests/smoke_runtime_api.py` and the approved host smoke path (`scripts/smoke_api.sh` via `scripts/smoke_postgres.sh`), so the surface is now explicitly test-backed.

capabilities.name: operator_db_visibility_and_retention
capabilities.notes: Operator scripts explain DB size, retention, and pgdata footprint and are checked by release-asset and operator contract tests.

capabilities.name: smoke_and_release_gate_bundle
capabilities.notes: Local and CI smoke gates cover memory, Postgres, legacy migration, help-contract, and release-asset verification flows.

capabilities.name: postgres_contract_matrix
capabilities.notes: The host-side Postgres contract runner and CI job now cover artifacts, channel runtime, approvals, policy decisions, and task contracts beyond smoke-only API flows.

capabilities.name: rewrite_policy_disallowed_tool_api_coverage
capabilities.notes: HTTP-level coverage now asserts that rewrite contracts denying `artifact_repository` return `policy_denied:tool_not_allowed`.

capabilities.name: support_bundle_pgdata_attribution
capabilities.notes: Support bundles now capture expected volume/mount details plus live `ea-db` mount inspection output for pgdata investigations.

capabilities.name: external_action_policy_api_exposure
capabilities.notes: HTTP policy evaluation now exposes external-send approval checks directly, with docs/examples and API coverage backing queue item Q-167.

capabilities.name: artifact_lookup_api_exposure
capabilities.notes: Rewrite artifacts can now be fetched directly over HTTP, with README/RUNBOOK examples plus approved smoke-path coverage keeping the durable artifact store observable.

capabilities.name: receipt_and_run_cost_lookup_api_exposure
capabilities.notes: Execution proof records now have direct HTTP lookup routes, and both in-process tests and the approved host smoke path assert them.

capabilities.name: approval_resume_execution
capabilities.notes: Approving a paused rewrite now resumes the existing execution step and completes artifact/receipt/run-cost persistence instead of leaving the session stranded in an intermediate state.

capabilities.name: execution_queue_inline_worker
capabilities.notes: Rewrite execution now persists durable execution-queue rows, exposes queue state in session projections, drains queued work inline for API requests, and lets non-API runner roles lease queued work as workers.

capabilities.name: runtime_mode_fail_fast_storage
capabilities.notes: Setting `EA_RUNTIME_MODE=prod` now forces durable Postgres boot, rejects memory/auto fallback degradation, and is asserted by the approved Postgres smoke path.

capabilities.name: principal_request_context_guardrails
capabilities.notes: Principal-scoped connector and memory routes now derive scope from request context, fall back to `EA_DEFAULT_PRINCIPAL_ID`, reject caller-supplied mismatches with `403 principal_scope_mismatch`, and hide foreign connector updates.

capabilities.name: typed_step_handler_gateway
capabilities.notes: The rewrite scaffold now compiles and executes a typed three-step queue path (`step_input_prepare`, `step_policy_evaluate`, `step_artifact_save`) through explicit handlers instead of a thin artifact-save-only execution path.

capabilities.name: planner_dependency_graph_projection
capabilities.notes: Planner output now exposes dependency/input/output metadata for each rewrite step, and approval pauses on the actual approval-gated step after the non-side-effect prefix has already completed.

capabilities.name: planner_human_task_branch_projection
capabilities.notes: Task-contract metadata can now project a first-class `human_task` review branch into the plan graph via `budget_policy_json.human_review_role`, so review work is visible in planner output before runtime auto-execution lands.

capabilities.name: runtime_human_task_step_execution
capabilities.notes: Rewrite execution now auto-runs compiled `step_human_review` plan nodes into real human task packets, returns `202 awaiting_human` while the queue pauses, and resumes the remaining steps when the packet is returned.

capabilities.name: human_review_payload_artifact_override
capabilities.notes: When a compiled human-review step returns `returned_payload_json.final_text`, the downstream artifact-save step now persists that reviewer-edited text instead of the original source input.

capabilities.name: planner_human_review_operational_metadata
capabilities.notes: Task contracts can now project `priority`, relative SLA minutes, and `desired_output_json` onto compiled `step_human_review` nodes, and the runtime-created human task packet consumes that metadata directly for reviewer routing semantics.

capabilities.name: human_task_review_contract_metadata
capabilities.notes: Compiled `step_human_review` nodes and direct human task packets now carry explicit `authority_required`, `why_human`, and `quality_rubric_json` metadata, backed by durable storage and exposed in plan/session/operator projections.

capabilities.name: operator_profile_specialized_backlog_routing
capabilities.notes: Principal-scoped operator profiles now persist `roles`, `skill_tags`, and `trust_tier`, and backlog views can filter pending human tasks to only the work a given reviewer profile is specialized and trusted to handle.

capabilities.name: human_task_operator_assignment_hints
capabilities.notes: Human task payloads now compute reviewer routing hints from active operator profiles, rubric-derived skill tags, and trust-tier requirements so specialized operators can be suggested or preselected directly on each packet without a separate backlog scan.

capabilities.name: human_task_recommended_assignment_action
capabilities.notes: Human task assignment now accepts an omitted `operator_id` and consumes `routing_hints_json.auto_assign_operator_id` when a single exact reviewer match exists, so clients can apply the server-selected reviewer without resubmitting the same operator choice.

capabilities.name: planner_human_task_auto_preselection
capabilities.notes: Task contracts can now enable `human_review_auto_assign_if_unique`, which projects onto compiled `step_human_review` nodes and lets the queue runtime pre-assign a single exact reviewer match before the packet lands in the backlog.

capabilities.name: human_task_assignment_source_visibility
capabilities.notes: Human task packets now persist `assignment_source` so session/operator projections can distinguish manual assignment, route-level recommended assignment, and planner auto-preselection after later claim/return transitions.

capabilities.name: human_task_assignment_provenance_fields
capabilities.notes: Human task packets now persist `assigned_at` and `assigned_by_actor_id` so current reviewer ownership is timestamped and attributed across manual assignment, claim, return, and planner auto-preselection paths.

capabilities.name: human_task_assignment_history_api
capabilities.notes: Human task ownership transitions are now queryable via `GET /v1/human/tasks/{human_task_id}/assignment-history`, which filters the linked execution ledger down to `created`, `assigned`, `claimed`, and `returned` events so reassignment provenance survives current-state overwrites.

capabilities.name: session_human_task_assignment_history_projection
capabilities.notes: Session detail responses now include `human_task_assignment_history`, exposing the same ownership transition chain used by the task-scoped history route so operator UIs can audit assignment changes without a second fetch.

capabilities.name: human_task_assignment_history_filters
capabilities.notes: The task-scoped assignment-history route now accepts `event_name`, `assigned_operator_id`, and `assigned_by_actor_id` so operator tooling can isolate specific reassignment, claim, or return transitions without scanning the full chain.

capabilities.name: registry_backed_tool_execution_service
capabilities.notes: Tool-call rewrite steps now execute through a reusable `ToolExecutionService` backed by the tool registry, the built-in `artifact_repository` handler is registered automatically, and receipts/events expose normalized invocation metadata.

capabilities.name: connector_dispatch_tool_execution_slice
capabilities.notes: The built-in `connector.dispatch` handler is now wired into `ToolExecutionService`, exposed through `POST /v1/tools/execute`, and queues delivery outbox rows while returning normalized `tool.v1` receipt metadata.

capabilities.name: connector_dispatch_binding_scope_guardrails
capabilities.notes: `connector.dispatch` now requires an enabled connector binding in the caller's principal scope before `/v1/tools/execute` queues delivery, preventing foreign-scope or unbound dispatch attempts from producing side effects.

capabilities.name: approval_async_acceptance_contract
capabilities.notes: Approval-required rewrite requests now return `202 Accepted` with `session_id`, `approval_id`, `status=awaiting_approval`, and `next_action=poll_or_subscribe`, making the paused workflow state explicit at the API boundary.

capabilities.name: human_task_packets_kernel
capabilities.notes: Principal-scoped human task packets can now be created, listed, claimed, and returned over HTTP while staying linked to execution sessions/steps and surfacing in session projections plus host smoke and Postgres contract coverage.

capabilities.name: human_task_pause_resume_session_flow
capabilities.notes: Human task packets can now explicitly reopen a linked step into `waiting_human`, move the session to `awaiting_human`, and resume the step/session when the returned packet is posted back.

capabilities.name: human_task_operator_queue_filters
capabilities.notes: Human task queue listings now support role, assigned-operator, and overdue-SLA filters across both HTTP smoke coverage and the Postgres repository contract matrix.

capabilities.name: human_task_operator_backlog_endpoints
capabilities.notes: Operators now have direct pending-backlog and assigned-queue endpoints on top of the filtered human task store, and the smoke path asserts that claim actions surface immediately in those views.

capabilities.name: human_task_operator_assignment
capabilities.notes: Operator ownership can now be assigned explicitly while a human task remains pending, and the session ledger records `human_task_assigned` before later claim/return transitions.

capabilities.name: human_task_assignment_state_visibility
capabilities.notes: Human task payloads now persist and project explicit `assignment_state` values (`unassigned`, `assigned`, `claimed`, `returned`) so operator backlogs and session views can distinguish assignment lifecycle state without inferring it from generic pending status plus owner fields.

capabilities.name: human_task_last_transition_summary_projection
capabilities.notes: Human task list/detail/session rows now expose compact `last_transition_*` fields derived from the latest ownership event so operator UIs can triage the newest reassignment, claim, or return state without fetching the full assignment-history chain.

capabilities.name: human_task_last_transition_sorting
capabilities.notes: Human task list/backlog endpoints now accept `sort=last_transition_desc` so operator queues can order pending work by the freshest ownership change instead of default repository order.

capabilities.name: human_task_sla_sorting
capabilities.notes: Human task list/backlog endpoints now accept `sort=sla_due_at_asc` so operator queues can order pending work by the earliest pending SLA instead of default repository order.

capabilities.name: human_task_sla_transition_combined_sorting
capabilities.notes: Human task list/backlog endpoints now accept `sort=sla_due_at_asc_last_transition_desc` so operator queues can break same-SLA ties by the freshest ownership churn instead of default repository order.

env_contract.EA_APPROVAL_THRESHOLD_CHARS: rewrite inputs at or above this size require approval
